claim_text: All six strict transform families are state-transition-equivalent (byte-identical post-call snapshots over >15000 property cases, zero mismatch).
The only degrading transform (enum-opaque) drops Claude -0.20 (non-sig); it is INFORMATION-HIDING, not representation.
statistical_support: enum-legend (same codes, info restored) = 0.00 degradation -> the effect is hidden information, not representation; enum-opaque itself non-sig (CI incl 0)
limitations: representation-fragility thesis not supported on these models/tasks

claim_text: Degradation persists after matching information/content (representation, not information, is the cause).
statistical_support: NOT supported: restoring the legend (content) ERASES the effect (enum-legend=1.00). Supports Schema First (semantics not representation).
limitations: content-matching erases it -> central representation claim fails

claim_text: Model ranking reverses across equivalent interfaces (Kendall tau < 1).
statistical_support: NOT supported: <=0.10 enum gap within bootstrap noise; needs >=6 model families + Kendall-tau
limitations: 2 models cannot support a ranking claim

claim_text: A label-free STNF reduces representation-induced degradation on held-out transforms.
statistical_support: KILLED per pre-registered gate: content-matching erases the effect AND label-free STNF cannot recover hidden information from opaque codes. No representation-only degradation to recover. Method RETIRED.
limitations: STNF has nothing to recover -> method retired
